powerplant.title: POWER PLANT LIMITATIONS
powerplant.description: The Bell 206B3 is powered by an Allison Model 250-C20B or 250-C20J turboshaft engine. Limitations for the C20B are equally applicable to the C20J. The engine drives the main and tail rotors through a transmission system.
key_points: Allison 250-C20B and 250-C20J engines are interchangeable for limitation purposes
Takeoff power is limited to 5 minutes at 100% torque
Maximum continuous torque is 65%
Transient torque of 110% allowed for 5 seconds maximum; intentional use prohibited
TOT takeoff limit is 610°C for 5 minutes; maximum continuous is 738°C
TOT transient during power change is 810–843°C for 6 seconds maximum; intentional use prohibited
TOT during starting/shutdown is 610–927°C for 10 seconds maximum
N1 maximum is 105%; transient 106% for 15 seconds maximum
N2 minimum power-on is 97%; maximum is 100%
Use of throttle to control RPM is not authorized except in emergency

fuel_system.title: FUEL LIMITATIONS
fuel_system.description: The helicopter uses turbine fuel. Multiple fuel types are approved with temperature restrictions. Fuel boost pumps are required to be ON for normal operations. Unusable fuel quantity depends on boost pump status.
key_points: ASTM-D 1655 Type B or MIL-T-5624 Grade JP-4 approved at all ambient temperatures
Type A, A-1, JP-5, JP-8 limited to -18°C (0°F) and above unless aircraft has fuel pressure gage with red triangle at 6 PSI
Helicopters with fuel pressure gage red triangle at 6 PSI may use Type A/A-1/JP-5/JP-8 down to -32°C provided fuel pressure is 6 PSI or above
Helicopters with airframe-mounted fuel filter do not require anti-icing additive at any temperature
Both fuel boost pumps shall be ON for all normal operations
Operation with both fuel boost pumps inoperative is not authorized
Unusable fuel is 10 gallons with one or both boost pumps inoperative in unusual attitudes
Fuel pressure minimum 4.0 PSI; maximum 30.0 PSI
FUEL LOW warning illuminates at approximately 20 gallons (S/N 4110 and prior) or 17 gallons (S/N 4111 and subsequent)

specifications.item: FUEL LOW warning threshold (S/N 4110 and prior)
FUEL LOW warning threshold (S/N 4111 and subsequent)

specifications.notes: With one or both boost pumps inoperative in unusual attitudes or out-of-trim conditions

transmission_system.title: TRANSMISSION OIL LIMITATIONS
transmission_system.description: The main transmission and tail rotor gearbox use approved oils with defined pressure and temperature limits. Oil type is MIL-L-7808, MIL-L-23699, or DOD-L-65734.
key_points: Transmission oil pressure minimum 30 PSI; continuous 30–50 PSI; maximum 70 PSI
Transmission oil temperature continuous 15–110°C; maximum 110°C
Oil types MIL-L-7808, MIL-L-23699, and DOD-L-65734 (AS) approved
MIL-L-23699 and DOD-L-65734 limited to ambient temperatures above -40°C
Main driveshaft forward coupling has four Temp-Plates; black dot indicates overtemperature

rotor_system.title: ROTOR (NR) LIMITATIONS
rotor_system.description: The main rotor (NR) has defined RPM limits for powered and autorotative flight. Rotor RPM is monitored via a dual tachometer. A ROTOR LOW RPM warning light and audio alert activate below 90% with collective off the down stop.
key_points: Power on NR minimum 97%; maximum 100%
Transient rotor RPM droop to 96% permitted but should not exceed 5 seconds
Power off NR minimum 90%; maximum 107%
50% to 60% NR range: accelerate through as rapidly as practical with cyclic neutral
ROTOR LOW RPM warning activates below 90% NR when collective is off the down stop
Standard tail rotor diameter is 66 inches (P/N 206-016-201); 62-inch optional (P/N 206-010-750)

hydraulic_system.title: HYDRAULIC SYSTEM FAILURE
hydraulic_system.description: The hydraulic system provides power-assisted flight controls (cyclic, collective, and optionally pedals). In the event of failure, controls remain functional but require significantly increased force. An HYD BOOST (or CONTROL BOOST/HYDRAULIC SYSTEM) switch allows the system to be cycled as a reset.
key_points: Hydraulic boost failure first indicated by increased control forces
Feedback forces and rate limiting occur with hydraulics off
HYD BOOST circuit breaker should be cycled first if failure suspected
CONTROL BOOST or HYDRAULIC SYSTEM switch to be cycled as corrective action
Run-on landing at 12–17 MPH (10–15 knots) recommended after hydraulic failure
Maintain airspeed above translational lift speed for best control at touchdown

electrical_system.title: ELECTRICAL POWER FAILURE
electrical_system.description: Electrical power is provided by the engine-mounted starter/generator after engine start. There is no standby generator. Battery provides limited backup power. A loadmeter monitors generator output. Maximum loadmeter is 70%.
key_points: Starter used as generator after start is accomplished
No standby generator provision
Battery provides short-term backup in case of generator failure
GEN FAIL light indicates generator failure; GEN switch RESET then ON as first action
If generator failure persists, switch GEN OFF and reduce electrical load
Reduce altitude below 6000 feet pressure altitude if possible due to risk of fuel boost pump loss
Loadmeter maximum limit is 70%; normal load is 10–20%

specifications.notes: 60.0°C; turn BAT switch OFF and land as soon as possible
54.5°C; turn BAT switch OFF until light extinguishes

engine_anti_ice_system.title: ENGINE ANTI-ICE LIMITATIONS
engine_anti_ice_system.description: The engine anti-icing system uses bleed air or an electrically actuated valve to prevent ice formation in the engine air inlet. It must be ON in visible moisture below 4.4°C and must not be used above 4.4°C ambient. TOT rises when anti-ice is activated.
key_points: Engine anti-ice shall NOT be used above 4.4°C (40°F) ambient
Engine anti-icing shall be ON for flight in visible moisture below 4.4°C (40°F)
TOT will increase when ENGINE ANTI-ICING is switched ON
Switch placarded ENGINE DE-ICING on older helicopters; ENGINE ANTI-ICING on S/N 3567 and subsequent
PITOT HEAT switch to be ON in visible moisture below 4.4°C if installed
For operations in falling/blowing snow: Particle Separator (FMS-12), Deflector Kit (FMS-10), and Auto Re-Ignition (FMS-18) shall all be installed

specifications.notes: Anti-ice shall not be used above this temperature

specifications.item: Minimum OAT requiring anti-ice in visible moisture
specifications.notes: Anti-ice must be ON below this temperature in visible moisture

engine_starting_system.title: ENGINE STARTER LIMITATIONS
engine_starting_system.description: The engine is started using an electric starter motor. Starter duty cycles differ between external power and battery starts to prevent overheating. Starting is not recommended below 12% N1.
key_points: External power: 25 sec ON / 30 sec OFF cycles, three cycles then 30 min OFF
Battery power: 40 sec ON / 60 sec OFF cycles, three cycles then 30 min OFF
Throttle to flight idle at 15% N1 with TOT at or below 150°C
Start should not be attempted below 12% N1
Starter released at 56% N1
Minimum N1 start speed varies with OAT: 15% above 7°C, 13% at -16 to +7°C, 12% below -16°C
If main rotor not rotating by 25% N1, abort the start
Monitor TOT closely during start to avoid hot start (limit 927°C)

specifications.notes: Per cycle; 3 cycles then 30 min OFF
Per cycle; 3 cycles then 30 min OFF

specifications.item: Minimum N1 for start attempt (-16 to +7°C)

weight_and_balance.title: WEIGHT LIMITATIONS / LONGITUDINAL CENTER OF GRAVITY LIMITS / LATERAL CENTER OF GRAVITY LIMITS
weight_and_balance.description: The helicopter has defined gross weight limits for internal and external loading, with CG envelope varying with gross weight. Station 0 datum is 55.16 inches forward of the forward jack point centerline.
key_points: Maximum internal gross weight for takeoff and landing is 3200 lb (1451.5 kg)
Maximum external cargo gross weight is 3350 lb (1519.5 kg)
Loads resulting in gross weights above 3200 lb shall be on cargo hook only, not imposed on landing gear
Longitudinal CG range: station 106.0 to 114.2 inches (variable aft limit with gross weight)
With forward door(s) off: CG limited to station 106.0 to 110.0 inches
Lateral CG: 3.0 inches left to 4.0 inches right of centerline (varies with longitudinal CG)
Minimum front seat weight (pilot) is 170 lb (77.1 kg)
Station datum is 55.16 inches (1401.1 mm) forward of forward jack point centerline
components: Baggage compartment (max 250 lb / 86 lb per sq ft)

specifications.notes: Station 2900.7 mm; variable with gross weight

specifications.item: Longitudinal CG aft limit (forward door(s) off)

specifications.notes: Left of centerline; varies with longitudinal CG
Right of centerline; varies with longitudinal CG

airspeed_limitations.title: AIRSPEED LIMITATIONS
airspeed_limitations.description: Vne varies with gross weight, density altitude, and door configuration. Exceeding Vne with doors off causes cyclic fore and aft stick reversal and fuselage buffeting.
key_points: At 3000 lb and below: Vne 150 MPH IAS (130 KIAS) sea level to 3000 ft DA; decrease 4.0 MPH (3.5 KIAS) per 1000 ft above 3000 ft; max pressure altitude 20,000 ft
Above 3000 lb: Vne 140 MPH IAS (122 KIAS) sea level to 3000 ft DA; decrease 8.0 MPH (7.0 KIAS) per 1000 ft above 3000 ft; max density altitude 13,500 ft
85–100% torque takeoff power range: Vne 92 MPH IAS (80 KIAS)
Aft door(s) off: Vne 100 MPH IAS (87 KIAS) power on or off
Forward door(s) off: Vne 80 MPH IAS (69 KIAS) power on or off
Exceeding door-off airspeed limits causes cyclic reversal and fuselage buffeting
Maximum autorotation airspeed is 115 MPH (100 knots) IAS
Minimum descent airspeed in autorotation is 60 MPH (52 knots) IAS; maximum glide distance at 80 MPH (69 knots) IAS

specifications.item: Vne (≤3000 lb, sea level to 3000 ft DA)
Vne decrease rate (≤3000 lb, above 3000 ft DA)
Vne (>3000 lb, sea level to 3000 ft DA)
Vne decrease rate (>3000 lb, above 3000 ft DA)
Autorotation minimum rate of descent airspeed

altitude_limitations.title: ALTITUDE LIMITATIONS
altitude_limitations.description: Maximum operating altitude varies with gross weight. Below 3000 lb the limit is 20,000 ft pressure altitude; above 3000 lb the limit is 13,500 ft density altitude.
key_points: At 3000 lb gross weight and below: maximum operating 20,000 ft pressure altitude
Above 3000 lb gross weight: maximum operating 13,500 ft density altitude
Recommended to use approved oxygen equipment above 10,000 ft
Air starts prohibited above 12,000 ft

specifications.item: Maximum altitude for engine air start

stability_control_augmentation_system.title: STABILITY CONTROL AUGMENTATION SYSTEM (SCAS)
stability_control_augmentation_system.description: The optional SCAS Kit (206-706-305) provides stability augmentation in cyclic and yaw axes via sensor-amplifier, servo cylinders, and transducer. It can be manually overridden or disengaged at any time during flight.
key_points: System consists of sensor-amplifier unit, servo cylinders, transducer assembly, control head and panel
SAS PWR switch must be pushed in to activate; amber light illuminates, red light extinguishes after warm-up
SAS CYCLIC and YAW switches each have individual green lights
SAS REL switch on cyclic stick disengages system in flight
Erratic pitch, roll, or yaw oscillations: press SAS REL; land as soon as practical if condition persists
Hydraulic actuator failure (lost motion): press SAS REL; land as soon as practical with slow forward speed
All SAS switches to OFF prior to engine shutdown

particle_separator_system.title: PARTICLE SEPARATOR ENGINE AIR INDUCTION SYSTEM
particle_separator_system.description: The optional Particle Separator Kit (206-706-200 or 206-706-201) protects the engine from ingesting particles in blowing/falling snow conditions. It uses engine bleed air to purge the separator. Performance is slightly reduced compared to standard inlet due to increased pressure drop and bleed air usage.
key_points: Must be installed with Deflector Kit (FMS-10) and Auto Re-Ignition (FMS-18) for snow operations
Takeoff prohibited with any snow or ice in inlet or plenum areas
Ground operations and hover limited to 20 minutes total per occurrence
Ground idle limited to 5 minutes; if exceeded, shut down and inspect
Flight prohibited when visibility in falling/blowing snow is less than 0.5 statute mile
Particle separator more efficient at 100% RPM and hover power than at idle
Use basic helicopter performance data when separator is removed and screen reinstalled
Deflector baffles must be removed if particle separator is removed
components: Plexiglass inspection windows on inlet cowling

specifications.item: Ground/hover time limit per occurrence (snow ops)

specifications.notes: At idle power (twist grip at idle)

specifications.item: Minimum visibility for flight in snow

cargo_hook_system.title: CARGO HOOK 1500 POUND (681 KILOGRAMS) CAPACITY
cargo_hook_system.description: The optional cargo hook kit (206-706-335 or 206-706-101) provides external load carrying capability. It includes electrical and manual emergency release systems. Hooks are located near the helicopter CG at station 108.5 inches.
key_points: Maximum external cargo load (206-706-335): 1500 lb (681 kg)
Maximum external cargo load (206-706-101 and -104): 1200 lb (544.3 kg)
Maximum gross weight with external load: 3350 lb (1519.5 kg)
Vne above 3000 lb with external cargo: 91 MPH (78 KIAS)
Cargo hook location: station 108.5 in (2755.9 mm)
Simultaneous use of hoist and external cargo hook is prohibited
Discharge static electricity before attaching cargo sling
Cyclic CARGO RELEASE switch provides electrical release; EMER CARGO RELEASE PULL provides mechanical backup
Avoid critical relative winds during cargo operations
Bungee shock cord provides automatic stowing when hook not in use
A swivel link between suspension cable and hook is recommended (not supplied)
components: Frame and hook assembly (or A-frame mounts and crossbeam)
Electrical release (CARGO RELEASE switch on cyclic)
Manual emergency release (EMER CARGO RELEASE PULL handle)

specifications.item: Maximum gross weight with external load

emergency_flotation_system.title: HIGH-SKID GEAR WITH EMERGENCY FLOTATION / LIGHTWEIGHT EMERGENCY FLOTATION LANDING GEAR
emergency_flotation_system.description: Optional emergency flotation kits attach inflatable floats to the main skid panels. The floats are inflated by nitrogen bottle and can be armed manually or automatically (upon ENG OUT). Inflation time is approximately 5–6 seconds.
key_points: Float inflation time approximately 5–6 seconds
Maximum inflation airspeed: 70 MPH (61 KIAS) for standard kit; 60 MPH (52 KIAS) for lightweight kit
Vne with floats inflated (standard): 100 MPH (87 KIAS) all doors on; 60 MPH (52 KIAS) with aft door(s) off
Vne with floats inflated (lightweight): 80 MPH (69 KIAS)
Maximum autorotation airspeed with floats inflated (lightweight): 70 MPH (60 KIAS)
Maximum rate of climb with floats inflated: 1000 ft/min
Do not inflate floats more than 5000 ft (standard) or 2000 ft (lightweight) above anticipated landing surface
Undesirable pitching occurs during inflation above limit airspeed
Automatic arming: FLOAT POWER switch ON arms system when ENG OUT light illuminates
After emergency water landing: ferry airspeed restricted to 100 MPH (standard) or 80 MPH (lightweight) for repacking flight
Floats and covers must be installed for all flight operations
components: FLOAT POWER / FLOAT MANUAL ARM switch

specifications.item: Maximum rate of climb with floats inflated
Max altitude above landing surface for inflation (standard)
Max altitude above landing surface for inflation (lightweight)

hoist_system.title: HOIST - EXTERNAL
hoist_system.description: The optional external hoist kit (206-706-124 or 206-706-126) provides rescue/cargo hoisting capability. The winch is motor-driven at 50 ft/min cable travel rate with 100 or 110 ft of usable cable. Maximum hoist load is 300 lb.
key_points: 206-706-124: 100 ft (30.5 m) of usable cable; includes OVERHEAT WARNING light
206-706-126: 110 ft (33.5 m) of usable cable
Cable travel rate: 50 ft (15.2 m) per minute
Maximum hoist load: 300 lb (136 kg); refer to Hoist Loading Nomograph
Only 3 consecutive full cycles permitted; then 40 minutes cooling
Allow 30-second rest between each half cycle (full up or full down)
Object being hoisted must be completely in cabin before forward flight
Simultaneous use of hoist and external cargo hook is prohibited
Hoist operations are prohibited with standard fixed float gear (206-706-008)
Cable Guard Kit (206-706-214) required when hoist used with lightweight emergency floats
CABLE CUT switch available for emergency load release
Passenger operations with hoist installed are approved if hoist is not used and system is deactivated

specifications.notes: 136 kg; refer to Hoist Loading Nomograph
Full up and full down; then 40-minute cooling period

engine_auto_relight_system.title: ENGINE (AUTOMATIC) RE-IGNITION
engine_auto_relight_system.description: The optional Engine Relight Kit (206-706-038) provides automatic re-ignition if the engine flames out. Re-ignition is triggered by decay of engine bleed air pressure upon flameout when the system is armed. Required for snow operations in conjunction with Particle Separator and Deflector Kit.
key_points: Actuated by bleed air decay upon engine flameout
System must be ARMED (switch to center ARM position) before takeoff
ENGINE RELIGHT indicator light illuminates when re-ignition occurs; system remains armed for subsequent re-ignition
Do not activate above 12,000 ft pressure altitude
Must be installed with Particle Separator (FMS-12) and Deflector Kit (FMS-10) for snow operations
Switch has three positions: TEST (momentary up), ARM (center), OFF/RESET (down)
To extinguish re-ignition indicator: move switch to OFF/RESET then back to ARM
System shall be ARMED for all snow operations below 12,000 ft

specifications.notes: Pressure altitude; do not activate above this

bleed_air_heater.title: BLEED AIR HEATER
bleed_air_heater.description: The optional Bleed Air Heater (206-706-149 or 206-706-700) uses engine bleed air passed through a mixing valve to provide heated ventilation air to the cabin. A performance decrement applies when the heater is operating.
key_points: Engine bleed air flows from engine through bleed lines to mixing valve into cabin
HEAT/VENT switch is two-position (HEAT and OFF); VENT not operable
TOT increases with Bleed Air Heater operating; observe TOT limits
Must be switched OFF if engine failure (air start to be attempted), governor failure, or insufficient power
206-706-700 installed on S/N 3567 and subsequent; primary differences are ducting and air outlets
Hover performance is reduced when heater is operating; see performance charts for decrements
DEFOG levers available (206-706-149 only) for windshield defogging

environmental_control_system.title: ENVIRONMENTAL CONTROL SYSTEM (CABIN TEMP CONTROL)
environmental_control_system.description: The optional ECS Kit (206-706-344 or 206-706-402) is powered by engine bleed air and provides cabin temperature control (heating and cooling). Operation is prohibited during takeoff, hover, and landing.
key_points: Powered by engine bleed air; manually temperature controlled
Flight with ECS operating is prohibited during takeoff, hover, and landing
External cargo loading limited to 3200 lb (1451.5 kg) with ECS operating
ECS switch OFF before takeoff and landing; can be ON after translational lift attained
MAX HEAT position turns off unit cooling fan; do not use at ambient temperatures at or above -12°C
206-706-402 installed on S/N 3567 and subsequent
Allowable weight reduced by 75 lb when ECS is installed (placard on baggage door)

specifications.item: Max gross weight for external cargo with ECS operating

specifications.notes: Reduce allowable weight by this amount

specifications.item: Maximum OAT for MAX HEAT position
specifications.notes: Do not use MAX HEAT at or above -12°C to prevent damage

rotor_brake_system.title: DUAL ROTOR BRAKE SYSTEM
rotor_brake_system.description: The optional Dual Rotor Brake System (206-706-034) allows rapid deceleration of the rotor after engine shutdown. It consists of a hydraulic brake disc and dual brake assembly operated by a master cylinder handle.
key_points: Apply rotor brake between 38% and 30% rotor RPM
Avoid rapid engagement on ice or other slippery or loose surfaces to prevent helicopter rotation
Rotor brake handle must be in UP and LATCHED position before engine start
Rapid engagement can cause helicopter rotation on slippery surfaces

float_landing_gear_standard.title: FLOAT LANDING GEAR STANDARD TYPE (FIXED FLOATS)
float_landing_gear_standard.description: The standard fixed float kit (206-706-008) provides two streamlined multicell inflatable floats for water operations. Intentional power-off landings on land are prohibited. Maximum gross weight is 3000 lb.
key_points: Certified for water operations under day or night VFR nonicing conditions
Intentional power-off landings on land are prohibited
Maximum gross weight 3000 lb (1360.6 kg)
Vne 120 MPH (104 KIAS) sea level to 3000 ft; decrease 6.0 MPH (4.4 KIAS) per 1000 ft above 3000 ft
Vne 90 MPH (76 KIAS) with standard floats and only left forward door removed
Maximum operating altitude 16,000 ft pressure altitude
Hoist operations are prohibited and system shall be deactivated
Do not exceed 8000 ft altitude change from departure point without resetting float pressure
Maximum float inflation pressure 4.5 psig; minimum per Float Inflation Chart
Rate of climb reduced by 400 ft/min with standard float gear
Taxi at slow speed to prevent float bows nosing under
components: Position lights (relocated to float forward crosstube)

specifications.item: Maximum altitude change from departure without resetting pressure

flight_controls.title: FLIGHT WITH DOOR(S) OFF
flight_controls.description: The helicopter may be flown with various door configurations with specific restrictions. Protracted rearward and sideward flight is prohibited. Doors-off flight affects CG limits and Vne.
key_points: All unsecured items must be removed from cabin before flying with doors off
Protracted rearward and sideward flight prohibited
External cargo limited to 3350 lb gross weight with any combination of doors off
Flight with forward door(s) off is prohibited with litters installed
With only aft cabin door(s) off: no change to CG limits
With forward door(s) off or any combination including forward: CG limited to station 106.0–110.0
Minimum flight crew is one pilot operating from right crew seat
Dual controls required to use left crew seat as second pilot position

n2_overspeed_protection.title: N2 TRANSIENT OVERSPEED LIMITS
n2_overspeed_protection.description: Power turbine (N2) transient overspeed limits are defined graphically in figure 1-3. Intentional use of transient limits is prohibited. The limits correlate N2 overspeed percentage with maximum allowable duration.
key_points: Intentional use of N2 transient overspeed limits is prohibited
N2 transient overspeed limits defined in figure 1-3 correlating RPM to allowable duration
Maximum N2 transient overspeed duration is 15 seconds
During low power descents, RPM can be controlled by small collective increase and/or beeping down N2 governor
GOV RPM switch should be beeping to INCR as collective is applied

specifications.notes: Refer to figure 1-3 for RPM vs duration envelope

caution_warning_systems.title: CAUTION LIGHT (AMBER) SEGMENTS / WARNING LIGHT (RED) SEGMENTS
caution_warning_systems.description: The helicopter has a caution/warning light panel with red warning segments for critical conditions and amber caution segments for system malfunctions. Audio warnings accompany certain conditions.
key_points: ENG OUT: red warning with audio (intermittent) at N1 less than 55%
ROTOR LOW RPM: red/amber warning with steady audio when NR below 90% with collective off down stop
BATTERY HOT: red warning at 140°F (60°C); turn BAT OFF, land ASAP
BATTERY TEMP: amber caution at 130°F (54.5°C); turn BAT OFF until extinguishes
TRANS OIL PRESS: amber caution; land as soon as possible
TRANS OIL TEMP: amber caution; reduce power, check pressure, land ASAP
ENGINE CHIP, TRANS CHIP, T/R CHIP: land as soon as possible
GEN FAIL: reset GEN switch; if persists, GEN OFF and land as soon as practical
FUEL PUMP: one or both boost pumps inoperative; descend below 6000 ft if possible
FUEL FILTER / AF FUEL FILTER: land as soon as practical; clean before next flight
BAGGAGE DOOR: land as soon as practical
FUEL LOW: ~20 gal remaining (S/N 4110 and prior) or ~17 gal (S/N 4111 and subsequent)
WRN HORN MUTE button (if installed) can mute audio warnings

specifications.item: ENG OUT audio activation N1 threshold
specifications.notes: Below this N1 triggers ENG OUT audio
NR below 90% with collective off down stop
